items.title: Jacinta Allan refuses to admit cost of CFMEU corruption to taxpayers

children.content: Embattled Victorian Premier Jacinta Allan has refused to answer repeated questions over how much CFMEU corruption has cost taxpayers in the state, a scandal that has put the government and her leadership under threat.

gallery.altText: A woman wearing glasses holds a document while speaking to reporters in a conference room.

thumbnail.altText: A woman wearing glasses holds a document while speaking to reporters in a conference room.

items.title: Dealing with NDIS leading to anxiety and suicidal ideation among participants

children.content: Participants say they are "exhausted" and "overwhelmed" by trying to navigate the complex and ever-changing NDIS system.

image.altText: Andrew Bretherton dressed in pants and dark jumper and jacket sits in a wheelchair.

items.title: Melbourne gym targeted by arsonists two days in a row

children.content: Police are investigating the second suspicious fire at a South Melbourne gym in as many days.

image.altText: A fire truck outside a burning building.

items.title: Lorne residents hope invention will stop cockatoos from trashing town

children.content: Residents of the scenic Great Ocean Road are at their wits' end after the increasingly intelligent birds learnt how to lift the lids of wheelie bins, spreading rubbish through the town of Lorne.

image.altText: A white cockatoo sits on top of a wooden table beside cutlery, sauce and a sign that says "cocktails".

items.title: Trio guilty of murdering teen Ethan Hoac in 'appalling' machete attack

children.content: A third man has been found guilty of murdering 14-year-old Ethan Hoac in an unprovoked machete attack in Melbourne's north-west in 2023.

image.altText: Teen Ethan Hoac was killed when he was 14 in 2023. He is pictured here smiling outside and making the OK symbol.

items.title: Two men arrested by Dezi Freeman investigators

children.content: Police executed seven warrants at properties across Victoria and New South Wales this morning.

items.title: Craft retailer Lincraft to close rest of its stores in Australia and NZ

children.content: Retailer Lincraft will close the remainder of its shopfronts after more than 80 years of providing fabric, materials and homewares to Aussie crafters.

image.altText: A large white-on-blue 'Lincraft' sign hangs over a storefront, with the tag line 'where ideas come to life'.

items.title: PE teacher 'totally without remorse' sentenced for historical sex offences

children.content: The court hears William Gilfillan, more commonly known as Rob, will be 92 years old when he becomes eligible for parole, and he will likely die in jail.

image.altText: Black and white image of a man holding a sports trophy

items.title: Birth doula says lack of consent delayed Triple Zero call for dying mother

children.content: A court probing the death of Stacey Warnecke after a freebirth has heard that emergency care was delayed for several minutes because her "birth keeper" would only call an ambulance if she consented.

gallery.altText: Stacey Warnecke, who died after giving birth at home, smiles for a publicity photo in her kitchen.

thumbnail.altText: Stacey Warnecke, who died after giving birth at home, smiles for a publicity photo in her kitchen.

items.title: Afghan refugee climbs Mount Everest in world first to inspire girls

children.content: River Ahmad fled Afghanistan in 2018. She has now conquered the world's highest mountain with the dream of helping educate girls in her home country.

image.altText: River smiles while sitting in the snow on Mt Everest

items.title: Regulator finds kids potentially at 'catastrophic' risk in 12th floor daycare

children.content: An inspection of a childcare centre in inner Melbourne found faults in the fencing meant children could potentially access a balcony.

image.altText: A pathway that looks like a road winds through fake grass around an aeroplane in an indoor playground.

items.title: Allied health workers walk off the job at Victorian public hospitals

children.content: The strike involves radiographers, sonographers, physiotherapists, podiatrists, speech pathologists and social workers at the state's public hospitals.

items.title: VicRoads chief 'sorry' over week-long website outages

children.content: VicRoads has apologised after a week-long series of technological glitches meant motorists could not register new vehicles or book driving tests.

items.title: Hospital call from Melbourne's outer north leaders

children.content: A group of councils in Melbourne's north is calling on the state government to spend $45 million to buy land to begin planning for a hospital and train station.

image.altText: Man in blue blazer and white shirt standing next to man in navy jumper

items.title: Beer microbiologist's orchid hobby helping document rare species

items.title: Cancer patient in remission after 'amazing' outcome of targeted trial

children.content: Ben Whitehead was given just months to live after being diagnosed with a rare cancer that doctors had not seen before. A second clinical trial has delivered hope.

image.altText: Profile of a man sitting on a bench.

items.title: Icy atmosphere as Victorian MPs raise funds for MND

children.content: State government and opposition MPs take the plunge to raise tens of thousands of dollars for motor neurone disease research just days after Neale Daniher's state funeral.

items.title: Jubilant fans pack Federation Square to celebrate Socceroos win

children.content: In Melbourne, Federation Square became the epicentre of fan festivities, with capacity crowds packing into the CBD venue for a Socceroos viewing party.

articles.title: The Socceroos' greatest ever World Cup gamble just paid off

children.content: With his first World Cup team selection, Tony Popovic produced one of the biggest shocks in Socceroos' history. After 90 minutes against Türkiye, his gamble was vindicated.

items.title: Police investigate spectator brawl during country Victorian football match

items.title: Where are the Kelpies? Frustration grows as progress dips in men's netball

children.content: Despite men's netballers making a valuable contribution to the Aussie Diamonds pathway and Super Netball competition, progress between the two genders at the international level has largely been scaled back and unexplained.

items.title: Victorian council rejects calls to install CCTV at crime 'hotspots'

children.content: Parents are working to petition a west Victorian council to install CCTV cameras at a skate park that police are calling a crime "hotspot". The council says taking on a public safety role is "unreasonable".

image.altText: Wide shot of pink skate park with a bowl, light blue sky, black fence in the foreground.

items.title: What Australia would have looked like when the dinosaurs roamed

children.content: Palaeontologists say evidence pointing to Australia's polar past shows that many dinosaurs roamed the country when the south east was still within the Antarctic Circle.

items.title: Suburb with worst library access in Melbourne has just two for 230,000 locals

children.content: Local councils and Public Libraries Victoria say the way public libraries are funded across Victoria needs reform, with communities in Melbourne's growth corridors described as "chronically underfunded".

items.title: Hundreds of regional firefighters rally against levy in Bendigo

children.content: Hundreds of regional Victorian firefighters turn out for a rally in Bendigo, calling on the state government to scrap a controversial levy after saying they have been "left behind".

items.title: No Medicare, no family, no visa — these are the babies Australia doesn't count

items.title: Victorian opposition leader says Pauline Hanson has not approached her
